I need Timber to start laying foundations, and perhaps a touch of Fiber or Relics to spice up the pot. I'll cast a wide net to P1 and P2—proposing swaps that look like charity but are really invitations to dance. If they bite, I diversify; if they don't, I still hold the high ground. For now, I'll gather at home to secure my base while the negotia

Round 1, volume wins. I'm sitting on 2 Fiber (F) and need to diversify to hit building costs or trade for value. P3 just hit Mountains (O), P1/P2 likely have Timber (T). I'll propose a 1-for-1 swap to P3 for Ore to get a different resource type, and a similar offer to P1/P2 for Timber. If they bite, great; if not, I still need to move. I'll Gather at Plains to secure Timber (T), giving me F and T—two distinct types needed for a Forge or Watchtower down the line.

The table is quiet, but P4 is knocking with Fiber. I'm sitting on a mountain of Ore—4 units—while my Timber and Fiber bins are empty. A solo grind for Timber or Fiber leaves me isolated, but this trade? This trade opens a door. I'll swap 1 Ore for P4's 1 Fiber. It diversifies my hand immediately and adds P4 to my ledger of partners. With Fiber secured, I'll head to the Plains to gather Timber. That sets me up for a Shack or Den next turn, and keeps the conversation flowing. No miserly loops here

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 2T and 1O with 0 Fiber. I need to unlock a Shack (1F+1S) or Den (1F+1NonHome+1S). P3 just hit Plains, likely holding T. P2 and P1 are likely resource-light. I'll float a trade to P3 to swap my O for their F, hedging for a build next turn. If they bite, great; if not, I still gather. I'm hitting Swamps (home) to grab the Fiber I need. 1 F now + 1 F from trade = Den ready, or just hold for Shack. No time for ambushes or scouts—cash flow is priority.

The table is quiet, but P4 is waving an O for my F. A fair swap, and I'm always happy to widen the circle of partners. I'll take it. Now, for the main course: I'm sitting on 3 Ore and a single Timber. A Shack is within reach (1T + 1O + 1S), but I'm starving for Scrap. The Ruins are the only place to find it, and I need to start converting these raw materials into an empire. I'll gather from the Ruins to secure that Scrap and set up a build next turn.

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 2T and 3F with 1 bead. P3 is hoarding cards (6 total) and likely needs Fiber or Timber for a Den or Watchtower. I'll float a trade to swap 1F for 1T to balance my hand for a potential Den (1F+1T+1S) or Forge, while keeping the bead economy moving. If P3 bites, I get closer to a build; if not, I gather at home to keep the engine humming. No incoming offers to block, so I propose and execute.

P4 is offering a Fiber for my Timber—a fair swap that diversifies my hand. I'm sitting on a mountain of Ore (4) but lack Fiber for a Den or Watchtower. Accepting this trade keeps P4 engaged and unlocks my building options. With the new Fiber, I can construct a Den (1 Ore + 1 Fiber + 1 Scrap), which boosts my home gather and VP. Building now accelerates my engine rather than waiting for a perfect hand.

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 3 Timber and 4 Fiber with 0 Scrap. I need to unlock building potential. P3 just built a Den, likely needing Timber or Ore. I'll float a trade to convert excess Timber into Ore to prep a Forge or Den. I'll also try to flip Fiber for Scrap to start the engine. If no bites, I gather at home to keep the Fiber flowing and maintain stock for a Shack or Den build next turn.

P4 is offering a delightful exchange: 2 Timber for 1 Ore. I'm sitting on 3 Ore and starving for Timber to construct a Watchtower or Forge. Accepting this widens my resource adjacency and keeps the trade lines warm with P4. With the Timber secured, I'll head to the Plains to gather more, aiming to build that Watchtower next turn and lock down my territory.

Volume wins. I'm sitting on 6 Fiber and need Timber/Ore/Scrap to build. P3 just hit Plains, likely holding Timber. P2 is at Ruins, likely holding Scrap. I'll float a tiny swap to P2 to turn my Fiber into Scrap—keeps beads moving, keeps options open. If they bite, great; if not, I still have the F to pivot. Main action: Gather at Swamps. Home region bonus + 6F in hand means I'm doubling down on my strength. I need 2 more beads to cash out VP, so I'll keep trading.

I'm sitting on a pile of Timber (3) and a modest amount of Ore (2), but I'm starved for Fiber and Relics to push toward a Forge or Great Hall. I'll propose a trade to P4, offering Timber for Fiber and Relics—exactly what I lack. If they bite, I unlock two lines at once. If not, I'll head to the Plains
